testcase.name: test_arbitrage_scan

failure.message:
AssertionError: The preview environment is not responding. It may be starting up.
assert 502 == 200 + where 502 = <Response [502]>.status_code

testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_omnilegal_v4_sota.py:63: in test_arbitrage_scan assert r.status_code == 200, r.text E AssertionError: The preview environment is not responding. It may be starting up. E E assert 502 == 200 E + where 502 = <Response [502]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_diff_compare

failure.message: assert None + where None = <built-in method get of dict object at 0xff81efa028c0>('used_model') + where <built-in method get of dict object at 0xff81efa028c0> = {'counts': {'added': 0, 'removed': 0, 'reworded': 1, 'unchanged': 0}, 'diff_chunks': [{'kind': 'reworded', 'left': 'Section 124A. Whoever brings the Government into hatred shall be punished.', 'right': 'Section 152. Whoever endangers sovereignty shall be punished.'}], 'impact': {'authority_changes': [{'note': "The actus reus element shifts from a subjective emotional standard (causing 'hatred' toward government) to an objective harm standard (endangering the state's 'sovereignty'). This changes what prosecution must prove and narrows the range of speech/conduct caught.", 'quote_left': 'brings the Government into hatred', 'quote_right': 'endangers sovereignty'}, {'note': 'The provision is renumbered, indicating legislative reorganisation. This may affect cross-references in other statutes and procedural rules tied to the old section number.', 'quote_left': 'Section 124A', 'quote_right': 'Section 152'}, {'note': "The protected legal interest changes from 'the Government' (the incumbent administration or state apparatus) to 'sovereignty' (the supreme authority and constitutional integrity of the state itself). This elevates the threshold and changes the nature of harm from political to constitutional.", 'quote_left': 'the Government', 'quote_right': 'sovereignty'}], 'defences_change': 'No defences are articulated in either LEFT or RIGHT text. No change can be identified.', 'obligation_change': "The prohibited conduct changes fundamentally. LEFT obligated persons to refrain from acts causing 'hatred' toward 'the Government' (an emotional and political standard). RIGHT obligates persons to refrain from acts 'endanger[ing] sovereignty' (a constitutional and territorial standard). The RIGHT formulation is arguably narrower in application but potentially broader in who may be considered to endanger sovereignty beyond direct criticism.", 'penalty_change': "Both LEFT and RIGHT state offenders 'shall be punished' but neither excerpt specifies the quantum or type of penalty. No penalty change can be determined from the provided text.", ...}, 'left_label': 'IPC 124A', ...}.get

testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_omnilegal_v4_sota.py:190: in test_diff_compare assert j.get("used_model") E assert None E + where None = <built-in method get of dict object at 0xff81efa028c0>('used_model') E + where <built-in method get of dict object at 0xff81efa028c0> = {'counts': {'added': 0, 'removed': 0, 'reworded': 1, 'unchanged': 0}, 'diff_chunks': [{'kind': 'reworded', 'left': 'Section 124A. Whoever brings the Government into hatred shall be punished.', 'right': 'Section 152. Whoever endangers sovereignty shall be punished.'}], 'impact': {'authority_changes': [{'note': "The actus reus element shifts from a subjective emotional standard (causing 'hatred' toward government) to an objective harm standard (endangering the state's 'sovereignty'). This changes what prosecution must prove and narrows the range of speech/conduct caught.", 'quote_left': 'brings the Government into hatred', 'quote_right': 'endangers sovereignty'}, {'note': 'The provision is renumbered, indicating legislative reorganisation. This may affect cross-references in other statutes and procedural rules tied to the old section number.', 'quote_left': 'Section 124A', 'quote_right': 'Section 152'}, {'note': "The protected legal interest changes from 'the Government' (the incumbent administration or state apparatus) to 'sovereignty' (the supreme authority and constitutional integrity of the state itself). This elevates the threshold and changes the nature of harm from political to constitutional.", 'quote_left': 'the Government', 'quote_right': 'sovereignty'}], 'defences_change': 'No defences are articulated in either LEFT or RIGHT text. No change can be identified.', 'obligation_change': "The prohibited conduct changes fundamentally. LEFT obligated persons to refrain from acts causing 'hatred' toward 'the Government' (an emotional and political standard). RIGHT obligates persons to refrain from acts 'endanger[ing] sovereignty' (a constitutional and territorial standard). The RIGHT formulation is arguably narrower in application but potentially broader in who may be considered to endanger sovereignty beyond direct criticism.", 'penalty_change': "Both LEFT and RIGHT state offenders 'shall be punished' but neither excerpt specifies the quantum or type of penalty. No penalty change can be determined from the provided text.", ...}, 'left_label': 'IPC 124A', ...}.get

testcase.name: test_voice_verify_chunk

failure.message: AssertionError: claims len=0 assert 0 >= 1 + where 0 = len([])

testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_omnilegal_v4_sota.py:300: in test_voice_verify_chunk assert len(claims) >= 1, f"claims len={len(claims)}" E AssertionError: claims len=0 E assert 0 >= 1 E + where 0 = len([])
